programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: DiNozzo teams up with his girlfriend, ATF agent Zoe Keates, to investigate the circumstances surrounding the death of a US Marine in what initially seems like a simple road accident. The team reveals the dead man did indeed die due to a broken neck sustained during a crash, but someone shot at him first. As DiNozzo and Keates pursue their inquiry, they discover the victim's death is connected to a controversial ATF plan to place traceable firearms in the criminal system, and then follow them to the heads of crime organisations. Naval crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US marine visits the home of his former captain, only to find the man has been killed, and the killer is waiting inside the property. While the soldier escapes with his life, the attacker gets away too. It quickly emerges the prime suspect is Qasim, an Afghan translator evidence, and evidence points against him, until it is discovered he has a brother, who is loyal to the Taliban. The subsequent chain of evidence leads Gibbs and Bishop to Afghanistan(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Naval intelligence ensign is killed in an unusual car accident. Gibbs and his team are reunited with old friend Ned Dorneget, who is now working with NCIS cyber operations, and NSA attorney Jake Malloy to investigate the case, and discover a conspiracy involving terrorist recruitment on the internet(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Special Agent Ned Dorneget of the NCIS cyber division joins the team to further their investigation into The Calling, the insidious terror network radicalising youngsters via the internet. When it emerges young suicide bomber Bradley was not, in fact, affiliated with The Calling, Gibbs and his colleagues realise they might be back to square-one. Determined to bring the organisation down, the agents pursue two new leads, an arms dealer who may be supplying The Calling with their signature German S-mines, and another boy given a deadly mission by the terrorists. Guest starring Matt Jones(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The death of Special Agent Ned Dorneget hits the team hard, and when Gibbs receives a visit from his late colleague's mother - CIA Officer Joanna Teague - he must decide whether to let her help with the investigation into the Calling. Meanwhile, Gibbs hopes his young charge Luke, formerly one of the Calling's radicalised footsoldiers, might be key to bringing the organisation down. However, when the terrorists discover where their defecting comrade has taken refuge, the abduct him and flee to Iraq. The team follows hot on their heels, but it seems Luke's ties to the Calling might be stronger than anyone at NCIS ever anticipated. Naval crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, with guest Mimi Rogers(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team helps Cabe when he receives a distress code from his ex-wife Rebecca. programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Great Britain 400m hurdler Eilidh Doyle, Burnley goalkeeper Nick Pope, netball star Pamela Cookey and England rugby union international Zach Mercer join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell return for an episode of previously unseen material from the hit comedy panel show. Among the participants setting out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves are Clare Balding, Jay Blades, Liz Bonnin, Asim Chaudhry, Victoria Coren Mitchell, Greg Davies, Greg James, Sue Johnston, Guz Khan, Alice Levine, Steph McGovern, Stephen Merchant, Bob Mortimer, Sharon Osbourne, Richard Osman, Jennifer Saunders, Ranj Singh, Joe Sugg, Claudia Winkleman and Lucy Worsley(n)

programme.title: King Gary
programme.desc: The family heads to the coast for a holiday, with Gary keen to enjoy some quality time with Terri. However, she is obsessed with making holiday pals and befriends a French woman whose less-than-friendly husband has nothing in common with Gary - who finds himself relegated to looking after the kids while everyone else has fun. Big Gary is excited about getting a pasty from a shop he visited years ago, but can't quite remember its location, while Denise's fear of seagulls prevents her going down to the sea front to help. Comedy, starring Tom Davis, Laura Checkley, Simon Day and Camille Coduri(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by singer, actor and producer Justin Timberlake and Oscar-nominated actress Anna Kendrick, who are both part of the voice cast of animated sequel Trolls World Tour. Singer-songwriter Sam Smith also appears, alongside comedian Alan Carr and Strictly and The Greatest Dancer star Oti Mabuse. Plus, more intrepid audience members attempt to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair(n)

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The queens' creative marketing skills are put to the test as they are tasked to create an advert and brand themselves. American drag queen Katya makes a surprise visit, while pop icon Cheryl joins Michelle Visage and Graham Norton on the judging panel(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: National and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45. Presented by Jon Kay and Tina Daheley(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Andrew Wong and Cyrus Todiwala, and a special guest faces his or her food heaven or hell(n)

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: The chef demonstrates how to make healthy dishes full of exciting flavours and textures, including Spanish-style squid and a chickpea and chorizo stew. Meanwhile, the group are invited to take part in a martial arts session, before Bev persuades the gang to try out her favourite workout, a vigourous dance class called Clubbercise. Later, Tom invites the whole gang over for lunch, where he prepares an easy chicken tray-bake, full of aromatic Moroccan flavours(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look back at last night's Premier League fixture between Wolverhampton Wanderers and Leicester City and preview Norwich City v Liverpool(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Thomas Plant help two teams find items at the Three Counties Showground in Malvern, Worcestershire, while presenter Eric Knowles oversees proceedings(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents live coverage of the prestigious meeting from Emirates Arena. World heptathlon champion Katarina Johnson-Thompson and Scottish favourite Laura Muir are among the stars scheduled to compete, with the latter aiming to break the world record in concluding event of the day - the rarely run 1,000m. With analysis from Denise Lewis, Colin Jackson and Paula Radcliffe, and commentary by Steve Cram, Andrew Cotter and Steve Backley(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: Doctor and television presenter Ranj Singh, former TOWIE cast member Jessica Wright, author and former politician Alan Johnson and comedian and writer Katy Brand brave the famous black chair answering questions on RuPaul's Drag Race, the character Monica Geller from Friends, pop group the Beatles and the movie Dirty Dancing. John Humphrys presents(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special experts edition of the general knowledge quiz. Contestants are Dr Zoe Williams, Dr Hilary Jones, Joe Swift, Rachel De Thame, Francis Wilson, Alex Beresford, Linda Barker and Judi James(n)

programme.title: The Greatest Dancer
programme.desc: Alesha Dixon and Jordan Banjo host the dancing contest, featuring performers from a variety of styles, including ballet, jazz, street, ballroom, contemporary and disco. Tonight, the competition continues as the remaining eight acts take to the stage, competing for a chance to showcase their skills on Strictly Come Dancing and take home the £50,000 prize(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. Eleven contestants start each show and compete across a range of five play-along-at-home games that will see one of them leave with a cash prize of up to £10,000. Hosted by Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ruby and Violette's relationship is pushed to the limit and Harmony's life is put in danger. Will struggles to finish a tedious report for Connie and turns to Lara for help, but he soon learns he should not mix his personal life with his work. Elsewhere, tensions run high when Ethan and Rash are forced to work together(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Norwich City v Liverpool at Carrow Road and Southampton v Burnley at Saint Mary's Stadium. Liverpool will have expected to extend their lead at the top of the table with victory over the struggling Canaries, while the much-improved Saints were looking to avenge a 3-0 defeat at the hands of the Clarets on the opening day of the season. Plus, the key moments from Friday night's clash between Wolverhampton and Leicester City at Molineux(n)

programme.title: Eagle Eye (2008)
programme.desc: Two strangers find themselves on the run from the authorities when they receive mysterious phone calls informing them their lives are in danger. They are forced to follow the instructions of a woman who is tracking their every move, while at the same time trying to discover who is behind the conspiracy. Thriller, starring Shia LaBeouf, Michelle Monaghan, Rosario Dawson and Michael Chiklis(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman follows the Edwards family during a trial week in Perth as mum Shana tries to persuade reluctant husband Philip and their two older children, who are adamant about not going, that Australia is the life for them(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At the City Hall in Kingston-upon-Hull, Caroline Hawley uncovers a remarkable collection of suffragette postcards and David Harper is delighted by some Hull Daily Mail newspaper printing blocks, saved from the tip. Presented by Paul Martin(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches the Westerglen Transmitting Station in Falkirk, before visiting Gleneagles and Berry Town on route to his final stop on this leg, the city of Dundee(n)

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: It's the first full week of the challenge and Tom is back in Gloucester, the city where he grew up, to exercise and cook alongside his diet and fitness gang. As team chef, he wants to get them off to a good start on calorie counting - and familiar, comforting dishes is the way to go. Window cleaning brothers-in-law Dan and Alex volunteered for the challenge because of health concerns and a normal day sees them tucking into a big, calorific breakfast and grabbing fast food between jobs, but Tom joins the boys on a bike ride in the local countryside before giving them a tasty breakfast muffin to try(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: The host talks to the four Labour leadership hopefuls in a specially extended edition(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Andrew Neil and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Walks of Life
programme.desc: Mehreen Baig follows in the footsteps of Queen Victoria in the Cairngorms National Park, visiting a church that was built for the monarch but not completed until after her death(n)

programme.title: Greece with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part two of two. Simon travels from the Peloponnese peninsula into the mountainous north of the country. He meets local people including a group of rebel monks and conservationists caring for an injured bear, and discovers how they are coping with the political crises affecting Greece(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: With Sharon out of action, Robbie's fate is sealed. Niamh's bad behaviour results in betrayal and the Hennessys are desperate to protect themselves(n)

programme.title: Wild Brazil
programme.desc: Stephen Mangan narrates a documentary following capuchin monkeys, giant otters and coatis as they strive to raise their families. The first episode sees the young animals taking their first steps in some of the world's most extraordinary habitats, where they have to beware of deadly predators including the jaguar and caiman(n)

programme.title: Wild UK
programme.desc: The wildlife of the mountains, finding out about ravens, the golden eagles of the Scottish hills and asking whether the UK will ever again play host to a long-lost beast - the lynx(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Natasha Raskin Sharp are at Bristol Cathedral to assess a range of items, while presenter Paul Martin discovers the darker side of the city's trading history(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman, Vick Hope, Gethin Jones and Tim Vine test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey through south-east Asia, learning how coffee was first brought to Java for cultivation, visiting the headquarters of the Dutch East Indies Railway Company, and ending at Surabaya, a port bursting with local traditions, produce and culture(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Countryfile presenters Charlotte Smith and Tom Heap swap agriculture for antiques as they search Hampshire and Buckinghamshire for bargain collectibles, assisted by auctioneers James Braxton and Phil Serrell. Their final destination is an auction in Winchester - but before that, Tom turns woodworker as he learns the ancient craft of 'bodging' in the Chiltern hills, while Charlotte meets the famous Aylesbury duck - common to the area for 300 years and the inspiration behind Jemima Puddle-Duck(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Museum
programme.desc: Project manager Alice and her team unframe and scan a gallery of 500-year-old masterpieces - Raphael's cartoons, removing frames that have sealed them in for 30 years. While in the V&A's stunning jewellery galleries, newly-appointed keeper James must check some of the most fragile objects in the collection - the jewellery made with moving parts. Among the treasures are a spectacular butterfly ring worn by Beyoncé that appears to take flight with each gentle flex of the finger(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: Return of the documentary showing the challenges faced by the NHS, focusing on the work of seven trusts across Liverpool, whose hospitals stretch to north Wales, Cheshire and the Isle of Man. The city's new state-of-the-art hospital remains unfinished, three years after it was due to open, so two of the biggest hospital trusts are planning an ambitious merger. But this means that hundreds of patients and staff have to be transported across the city, while all the operating theatres bar one are closed(n)

programme.title: Mum
programme.desc: The family are stuck indoors as the weather takes a turn for the worse. Pauline receives a visitor and Cathy comes to an important decision. Comedy, starring Lesley Manville(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett(n)

programme.title: Win the Wilderness: Alaska
programme.desc: As winter draws in and the weather grows wilder, the remaining couples are challenged to clear a path through a dense area of the wilderness for a dog sled team in just three hours. Once the work is completed, the captain of the sled team selects one of the couples to visit Ose Mountain, where they must clear debris from a reservoir and learn how to correctly butcher an animal in the wild(n)

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: A look at the smaller boats that fish off Cornwall's coast, often with only a single crew member. Despite being a sustainable method of fishing, this traditional approach is coming under threat from industrialisation, a lack of new talent taking up the trade, and declining stocks of mackerel and other once common fish. In Penberth, James is the only fisherman under 65 - and since traditional methods rely on boats supporting each other, he may have to give up when the older generation retires. On the Helford River, a veteran fisherman shares his concerns for the fish processing industry if Britain leaving the EU leads to a decline in foreign labour(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood spends 24 hours working as a firefighter in Texas, but just getting the gear on in time is a tough job for him, let alone confronting his fear of heights(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: Michael Mosley and a team find out if eating less food can boost your memory, and Dr Javid Abdelmoneim investigates over-the-counter cannabis-based health products. Elsewhere, Zoe Williams reveals how to spot a hidden illness that can cause stillbirth, and Alain Gregoire discovers the body's secret built-in stress reliever(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Carl and Danielle want to move to Australia but a health scare has shown them how fickle life can be. A week in Perth helps them decide whether they could leave their family behind(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a pair of teachers with a £600,000 budget who want to upsize and find the ideal country home for them and their four young children. Away from property, Jonnie turns the heat up on his Lancastrian tour when he visits one of the last working forges in the county(n)

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Michaela Strachan, Gillian Burke and Iolo Williams present a week of programmes live from the Cairngorms, showcasing wildlife stories recorded across the UK. The first episode features footage of pine martens, red squirrels, golden eagles and crested tits, and reveals what the current state of the mountainous region can reveal about the future of Britain's climate(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Carefree (1938)
programme.desc: A woman has doubts about her impending wedding and pays a visit to her fiance's therapist friend. However, the counselling does not go to plan and she develops romantic feelings for the doctor, forcing him to resort to hypnotism to cure her infatuation - with unforeseen consequences. Musical comedy, starring Fred Astaire, Ginger Rogers and Ralph Bellamy(n)

programme.title: Puttin' on the Ritz: The Genius of Fred Astaire
programme.desc: Fred Astaire's definitive performance of the song and dance routine, as recorded in the late 1920s by Irving Berlin, and later featured in his 1946 musical Blue Skies(n)

programme.title: Walks of Life
programme.desc: JB Gill explores the landscape of Dartmoor. Starting his journey in the shadow of the famous prison, JB encounters a beautiful stone church built by prisoners from the Napoleonic Wars and takes to a quad bike to herd sheep and Dartmoor ponies for a local farmer. Journeying out onto the moor itself, he learns how the expansive areas of peat bog need to be preserved in order to maintain the country's environment(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Rory has a grave secret and a bullet missing, so must lie to his grandfather, Jim. Sharon's aggressive return to the search for Robbie alarms Angela, while Patricia feels besieged(n)

programme.title: Wild Brazil
programme.desc: The animal families face the formidable monsoon rains and the giant otter cubs must watch out for predatory jaguars and learn to swim before their river floods. The coatis have to eat as much as they can ahead of their habitat disappearing underwater, while the youngest capuchin monkey needs to master some clever survival skills. Stephen Mangan narrates(n)

programme.title: Wild UK
programme.desc: Colin Stafford-Johnson and Lucy Cooke examine wildlife in cities. Colin travels to Newcastle to meet up with a seabird with a liking for high-rise living, while Lucy heads to Brighton to meet Dr Dawn Scott, who is unravelling the reasons behind the red fox's urban habits. Plus, how life for the people of Alaska can be governed by the arrival of the Pacific salmon(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At the City Hall in Kingston-upon-Hull, antiques experts Caroline Hawley and David Harper hunt for objects to take to auction. Caroline is entertained by the story behind some Laurel and Hardy autographs and David is gripped by a collection of postcards between young lovers from the Edwardian era. Presenter Paul Martin talks to playwright James Graham about the legacy of Hull's year as the UK's City of Culture(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman, Vick Hope, Gethin Jones and Tim Vine test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games, with the week's overall champion set to be crowned(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's 2,500-mile rail tour of south-east Asia reaches Malaysia. On the island of Penang, he traces the origins of the former British colony in Georgetown, rides one of the world's steepest funicular railways and is enchanted by exotic specimens at Malaysia's first butterfly sanctuary. Travelling on the nation's hi-tech railway network Michael visits its wettest town, Taiping, and learns how the discovery of tin made this land a prized possession for Britain. Next stop is the regal town of Kuala Kangsar, home to the Sultan of Perak and to one of Malaysia's most prestigious schools(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of the ordinary men and women of the Royal National Lifeboat Institution, who are ready to answer their pager's call and race to the rescue of those in trouble, 24 hours a day, 365 days a year, whatever the weather. Using footage shot on the crews' own cameras, the series captures the unpredictable work of the RNLI in unique detail(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: I'm a Celebrity comrades Stanley Johnson and Georgia 'Toff' Toffolo compete to find profit-making antiques in Cumbria, guided by auctioneers Paul Laidlaw and Izzie Balmer. Toff takes time out from shopping to learn about the importance boats have played in the Lake District and Stanley learns about the conversation work of artist John Ruskin(n)

programme.title: Our Coast
programme.desc: Adrian Chiles and Mehreen Baig explore Anglesey, the largest island in the Irish Sea. Mehreen recalls the story of the Menai Strait's magnificent bridges, while Adrian dons a wet suit to meet local paddleboarding legend Sian Sykes and see how she's playing a key role in helping rid the island of waste plastic. Plus, comedian Tudur Owen fulfils a boyhood dream by joining the Holyhead truckers at their brand new truck stop, Lucy Martin explores the shipwreck that changed the way weather is forecast and wildlife expert Patrick Ayree gets up close to the wild ponies of Newborough Warren(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig looks at 'Quads' and 'Quinns', inviting Nish Kumar, David Mitchell and Aisling Bea to join regular panellist Alan Davies to face her fiendish questions. As usual, the host doesn't particularly care whether the answers are right or not, so long as they are interesting(n)

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's Tour of Scotland
programme.desc: The comedian mingles among the crowds of the Edinburgh Fringe before heading to a bar where he meets author Val McDermid, learning her secret to creating realistic characters and dialogue. Travelling north, Frankie stays the night in a hotel linked to Dracula, prompting him to offer his own theory on what inspired Bram Stoker to create the famous vampire. Then, at Grampian Prison, he joins inmates learning about the Scots language. Finally, he performs his stand-up show in Aberdeen, alongside sign language expert Catherine King whose job it is to interpret his sometimes shocking routines(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Katie Razzall(n)

programme.title: Front Row Late
programme.desc: As London Fashion Week opens, Mary Beard and her panel of guests discuss the art and ethics of the clothing industry. Themes touched upon include when can fashion genuinely be regarded as an art form, and who is high-end designer fashion actually for?(n)

programme.title: End of Watch (2012)
programme.desc: Two Los Angeles cops make their regular patrol of the city, dealing with petty offences while one records their lives on camera for a documentary project. The officers end up in over their heads when they stumble on evidence that could bring down a drug cartel, putting them squarely in the gang's firing line. Crime thriller, with Jake Gyllenhaal and Michael Pena(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Callie Lewis was just 24 when she took her own life. She had been in the care of the NHS, but fell through the cracks of an understaffed and overstretched system. For the last 16 months, Panorama has been filming with her family as they tried to understand what went wrong. Shortly before she died, they learned that Callie was a member of an online suicide forum, where she found detailed advice on how to kill herself. As reporter Ellie Flynn discovers, Callie's death comes at a time when many people with mental health issues continue to complain about their struggle to connect with the services they need(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: From ancient Syria to present day Sheffield and out into the wilds of space, something is stalking the Doctor and her friends. As Graham, Yaz and Ryan return home to see friends and family, they find themselves haunted by very different experiences. Who is the figure calling from beyond the stars for help, and what are the fearsome Chagaskas terrorising Aleppo in 1380? To find the answers, the Doctor and her companions must embark on a mission that forces them to face their darkest fears(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood check out some mind-boggling armies from the natural world, including some sleepy suburbs invaded by 10 million bugs(n)

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Children's comedy panel show hosted by Iain Stirling, featuring studio-based frolics and slapstick challenges(n)

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Ten-year-old Caitlin wants to become a conservationist when she's older(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Daredevil wildlife presenter Steve Backshall is live in the studio to talk about his career(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall and the team head to Alaska, where they spot humpback whales and grizzly bears(n)

programme.title: Tomorrow's Food
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and a team of experts examine the cutting-edge technologies and produce transforming how food is grown, bought and eaten around the world. In the first edition, Dara heads to Kent to visit hi-tech greenhouse farm Thanet Earth, which grows a high quantity of fruit and vegetables throughout the year - and all without any soil. Chef Angela Hartnett tackles sell-by dates with the help of the US military, greengrocer Chris Bavin is in Australia to see the world's leading robotic farm and technology expert Shini Somara is in Shanghai to immerse herself in the restaurant of the future(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates this documentary following a couple of mountain lion families in Wyoming's Rocky Mountains. The dramatic story of two mothers struggling to raise their cubs is giving scientists an invaluable insight into the behaviour of these elusive predators(n)

programme.title: Our Coast
programme.desc: Adrian Chiles and Mehreen Baig explore Anglesey, the largest island in the Irish Sea. Mehreen recalls the story of the Menai Strait's magnificent bridges, while Adrian dons a wet suit to meet local paddleboarding legend Sian Sykes and see how she's playing a key role in helping rid the island of waste plastic. Plus, comedian Tudur Owen fulfils a boyhood dream by joining the Holyhead truckers at their brand new truck stop, Lucy Martin explores the shipwreck that changed the way weather is forecast and wildlife expert Patrick Ayree gets up close to the wild ponies of Newborough Warren(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman presents the semi-final, which sees the four remaining cooks produce a selection of party canapes and rustle up a dish from a tinned store cupboard staple. The ones who impress the least must fight for their place in the final by cooking again in the eliminator - and this week, Angela Hartnett gets them to produce a recipe also happens to be her husband's favourite dessert. Who will be hanging up their apron at the last hurdle?(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Extraordinary Homes
programme.desc: Architect Piers Taylor and property enthusiast Caroline Quentin explore unique homes in India, including a house built in the highlands outside of Mumbai, and another within the mountainous scenery of the Western Ghats. Elsewhere, the pair also visit a riverside bolthole drawing on Indian architectural heritage, before ending with a unusual building named 'Collage House'(n)

programme.title: Flatpack Empire
programme.desc: One of IKEA's newest designers, Hanna-Kaarina Heikkila, wants to challenge mass production by creating an 'imperfect vase' that has the finger-marks of its creator. She travels to China to see if her idea is possible. There's also a visit to the home of the Billy bookcase to see what it takes to be a supplier for IKEA, as the company tries to pitch a new range of wardrobes(n)

programme.title: Move Over, Darling (1963)
programme.desc: A woman returns home to America after five years marooned on a desert island, wanting to surprise her husband. However, she finds he has not only given her up for dead, but has also remarried and is enjoying a romantic honeymoon. Remake of vintage comedy My Favourite Wife, starring Doris Day, James Garner and Polly Bergen(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: An exploration of the romantic comedy, one of the most successful movie genres of all time, featuring interviews with on-screen couples including Doris Day and Rock Hudson, and Meg Ryan and Tom Hanks. The documentary also highlights how the rom-com has, over the years, given many female actors the opportunity to shine over their male co-stars, and looks at the 'Hugh factor' - and the impact of Richard Curtis's Four Weddings and a Funeral on Hollywood romance in the 21st century(n)

programme.title: 10 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Documentary following young dogs and their new owners over the course of their all-important first six months together. Telephone psychic Delia is keen on the idea of a big-breed dog to join her in her London flat, so canine behaviourist Louise Glazebrook arranges for her to try before she buys. Cocker spaniel Poppy joins the Vaughan family in leafy Surrey, where she gets the undivided attention of the children. However, three-year-old Isadora is a little too eager to play, even when the pooch wants to rest, and when Poppy bites back, Louise arrives with some advice on puppies and young children(n)

programme.title: Cities: Nature's New Wild
programme.desc: Animals that travel in and out of cities in search of food or a safe place for their young. African penguins have found the streets of Cape Town a safe environment from predators, while still providing easy access to their fishing grounds, while in St Lucia, hippos have developed a taste for eating residents' lawns. Black bears roam the streets of cities across North America, seeking out discarded food to help them consume the 20,000 calories a day they need to survive their six-month hibernation(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Sergeant Wilson inherits a title following the death of an elderly relative and joins the aristocracy - much to Mainwaring's annoyance. Classic comedy first aired in 1973, starring John Le Mesurier and Arthur Lowe(n)

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores British Columbia, discovering how two superpowers nearly came to war over a pig and joining the Royal Canadian Navy to firefight on board the frigate HMCS Regina. He also explores the rich British heritage and colonial past of Victoria - the provincial capital of British Columbia - discovers the origins of the Hudson's Bay Company, helps to carve a 36- foot totem pole and learns how to head off a charging bear(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig finds herself stuck in a 'Quagmire', as she invites Sindhu Vee, Sally Phillips and Aisling Bea to join regular panellist Alan Davies to face her fiendish posers. As usual, the questions are so difficult points are instead awarded for the answers the host finds most interesting as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Gina Yashere comperes the comedy show at London's Hammersmith Apollo. She warms up the audience before introducing routines by 2018 Britain's Got Talent winner Lost Voice Guy and Mock the Week regular Ed Gamble(n)

programme.title: Snowfall
programme.desc: Franklin finds a way of showing Officer Wright he can get to him, and begins expanding into other areas and working with other gangs - but that brings unpredictability. The CIA have big plans for Teddy's operation in Nicaragua, but his new Colombian contacts have stopped the supply - Teddy will need Julia's help moving out of his comfort zone to get things back on track(n)

programme.title: Snowfall
programme.desc: Alton tells Franklin some truths about what he can expect from his involvement with the CIA, so Franklin begins digging into his family history - and into Teddy's. Julia learns the reality of what Teddy has been doing out on his own without the agency's help, while Gustavo is setting everything up at the border(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: The Pennine Way
programme.desc: Polar adventurer Paul Rose heads for the North Pennines in the latest stage of his journey. He goes white-water rafting down the River Tees - and takes in one of Britain's best views at High Cup Nick. He also hears about a weather phenomenon unique to the Pennine Way and spends a night at a remote mountain refuge close to the highest point of the route(n)

programme.title: Walt Disney
programme.desc: The first of two documentaries exploring the life, career and legacy of Walt Disney, making use of archive footage alongside interviews with his friends, colleagues and relatives to present a portrait of a ruthlessly driven animator and entrepreneur whose single-minded ambition allowed him to create a string of cartoon masterpieces that would define an entire industry. This opening instalment charts Disney's early days, from the creation of Mickey Mouse through to the triumph of Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs, his first full-length animated feature film(n)

programme.title: Life Cinematic
programme.desc: The British director reveals the films that have influenced her life and career, including One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest, The Talented Mr Ripley, Brokeback Mountain and Claire Denis's French masterpiece Beau Travail. Sam also provides insight into the making of her most recent movie, A Million Little Pieces, and reflects on her early introduction to cinema, as well as her experience of moving to Hollywood to live and work(n)

programme.title: Inside Cinema: Meet the Family
programme.desc: Part one of two. A look at family in cinema in all their maddening, dysfunctional glory, with clans in Aliens, Back to the Future, Home Alone, Tokyo Story, Pan's Labyrinth and Gosford Park among those analysed. Kathy Burke narrates(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox reveals how the unique properties of light provide an insight into the origins and evolution of both mankind and the universe. He charts the importance of light in the development of the world's ancient civilisations, and explains how the speed of light allows the measurement of both distance and time - before pinpointing one of the most important moments in the evolution of life on Earth(n)

programme.title: How We Built Britain
programme.desc: David Dimbleby travels through Scotland, examining architecture unique to the area, such as Stirling Castle and the fairy-tale tower house of Craigievar. He visits the building that inspired Bram Stoker's Dracula in the Outer Hebrides, the granite city of Aberdeen and the tenements of Glasgow, home to one of Scotland's greatest architects Charles Rennie Mackintosh(n)

programme.title: The Silk Road
programme.desc: Sam Willis traces the history of one of the world's most famous trade routes, which ran from China's ancient capital Xian through the cities of central Asia and Persia. He begins his travels in Venice, examining how the architecture was shaped by Eastern influences, before arriving in China's ancient capital Xian where he discovers how a single deal to trade silk for war horses created a global network(n)

programme.title: Life Cinematic
programme.desc: The British director reveals the films that have influenced her life and career, including One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest, The Talented Mr Ripley, Brokeback Mountain and Claire Denis's French masterpiece Beau Travail. Sam also provides insight into the making of her most recent movie, A Million Little Pieces, and reflects on her early introduction to cinema, as well as her experience of moving to Hollywood to live and work(n)

programme.title: Love Songs at the BBC: A Valentine's Day Special
programme.desc: A selection of romantic songs for guilty pleasures, for courtship, for declarations of love, for looking someone in the eye and whispering sweet nothings, accompanied by a compilation of some of the greatest love songs from the likes of Celine Dion, Joe Cocker and Jennifer Warnes, Jason and Kylie, 10cc and Lionel Richie, all from the Top of the Pops era(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Mark Goodier and Andy Crane host the edition first shown on March 23, featuring performances by the Reynolds Girls, Alyson Williams, Donna Summer, Coldcut with Lisa Stansfield, Pat and Mick, Roachford, the Bangles, Kym Mazelle, Kim Wilde, Paula Abdul, Madonna and Bobby Brown(n)

programme.title: Classic Albums
programme.desc: The creation of the band's second album, which took the gothic synth-pop foundations of the band and combined them with arena-ready anthems leading to critical acclaim and the international hit singles Mothers Talk, Shout and Everybody Wants to Rule the World. With full co-operation of the band members, this documentary explores how the album was recorded, leaving its indelible imprint on New Wave(n)

programme.title: Roy Orbison: Love Hurts
programme.desc: Thirty years since the singer's death, another chance to see this documentary revealing his legacy as a beloved rock legend and a devoted father through intimate interviews with his three sons. Alex, Roy Jr and Wesley Orbison discuss the immense talent and fierce determination that provided the driving force behind their father's success and how his family life offered a strong spiritual base to escape the pressures of the rock ‘n' roll lifestyle(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the 70s 2
programme.desc: A celebration of some of the decade's most recognisable classics, including performances by the Carpenters, Bread, Charles Aznavour, 10cc, John Denver, Bellamy Brothers, Exile, Captain & Tennille and Dr Hook(n)

programme.title: Radio 2 in Concert
programme.desc: Highlights of a performance by the band at the BBC Radio Theatre in London. After forming as synth pop duo in the early 1980s, Roland Orzabal and Curt Smith achieved huge success on both sides of the Atlantic, and as a result of such a huge fan base over 30,000 people applied to be part of the audience for this intimate gig. Here, the boys from Bath perform their classic hits, including Mad World, Shout, Everybody Wants to Rule the World and Sowing the Seeds of Love(n)

programme.title: Sings Bacharach and David!
programme.desc: Archive performances of songs penned by Burt Bacharach and Hal David, featuring artists including Aretha Franklin, Dusty Springfield, the Carpenters, Sandie Shaw and the Stranglers. The programme also features Bacharach himself performing alongside Rufus Wainwright on Later with Jools Holland(n)

programme.title: Classic Albums

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads to Berlin, where history and the avant garde dwell side by side. A city that once made history with its divisive wall and Checkpoint Charlie now teems with an overwhelming array of innovative chefs offering delicious seasonal creations, such as pureed sunflower seeds, Jerusalem artichoke flans and crushed frozen pine nuts. Loved by the likes of Greta Garbo, Charlie Chaplin and Auguste Escoffier there's still a cornucopia of choice for those with a more traditional temperament with eisbein, meatballs and sausages topping every menu in the German capital(n)

programme.title: South Pacific

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Ball. Babies and their grown-ups play with balls and enjoy songs and story time together(n)

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Ashley Kendall, Aimee Campbell-Nottage, Donna Mullings and Simon Herdman present a poetry programme using a mix of sign language, the spoken word, music and animation to bring poems to life and capture the imaginations of both deaf and hearing children(n)

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: A story exploring why aardvarks have sticky tongues. Children's animation, based on an African folk tale(n)

programme.title: Apple Tree House
programme.desc: The children all take part in a sponsored silence as part of a school fundraising event, and as they get ever-more anxious to speak, they learn there are other ways to communicate(n)

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: With her parents stay at the hospital on the mainland awaiting the delivery of the new baby, Katie looks after the family cat, Fabbydoo, but he mysteriously disappears(n)

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: The adventurous duo plant some seeds after getting them for free in a newspaper(n)

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: Mum and Dad let Charlie go camping in the garden, and he is excited by the prospect of wild animals, survival and living off the land(n)

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: At bathtime, Tee wants one more game with his favourite toy Action Ape. Mo needs to find a way to get Tee into the bath and causes some bubble trouble(n)

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: It's a very cold day on the Clangers' Planet. The Iron Chicken has come down to the living cave to get warm. But when a Sky Moo wants to do the same, she finds the doors are frozen shut and she can't get in. So Granny has to come to the rescue with a clever plan to keep her and the other Sky Moos warm(n)

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Tombliboos ride on the Pinky Ponk, but it bumps into a tree and the gazebo. Narrated by Derek Jacobi(n)

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: JB Gill reads Amazing Daddy by Rachel Bright in a special episode for Father's Day(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: The investigators look into an IT support worker shamelessly helping himself to hospital computers and a gang illegally fly-tipping on an industrial scale(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development of a terraced house in Ramsbottom in Bury, a former shop in Gravesend in Kent and a house in Newcastle-under-Lyme(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Carl and Danielle want to move to Australia but a health scare has shown them how fickle life can be. A week in Perth helps them decide whether they could leave their family behind(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: The officers investigate the sale of knives to under-18s. They also catch up with a Lamborghini-driving fraudster who has so many aliases he loses track of who he is supposed to be(n)

programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: A visit from an old friend forces Karen to confront her past. A toy gun battle for Joe's birthday becomes a little too real and Bear tries to get in on Ayesha and Valerie's party(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu are hired to locate a photograph stolen from a local pub, whose owner is in the middle of a feud with another local family - only for events to take a deadly turn(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a house hunting couple find a country home with a budget of £700,000 in Buckinghamshire, and later visits a farm that produces high-end snail caviar(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Shrewsbury Food Festival plays host to first-generation farmer Sam, with his rare breed of pork, and the family run Kerry Vale Vineyard, selling their English wine(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The search reaches the New Forest, and after an encounter with some ponies, Tim Medhurst buys a horse, while Christina Trevanion turns farrier. Other items include a George III commode and some Scottish treasure(n)

programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: By Plaid Cymru(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones, Matt Baker and a team of roving reporters present topical stories from around the UK(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mick regrets admitting to Linda that Ollie might be better off without her, but before he can apologise his wife storms out. Whitney goes to the Slaters' to apologise to Kat, giving Leo the chance he needs to search for the letter. A night out for the Albert Square youngsters start badly as Dennis is less than pleased to see Bobby - and things quickly go downhill from there(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman presents the semi-final, which sees the four remaining cooks produce a selection of party canapes and rustle up a dish from a tinned store cupboard staple. The ones who impress the least must fight for their place in the final by cooking again in the eliminator - and this week, Angela Hartnett gets them to produce a recipe also happens to be her husband's favourite dessert. Who will be hanging up their apron at the last hurdle?(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A desert island survival course goes wrong when the leader - a trained survival expert and ex-SAS soldier - is found dead in the forest of a remote island near Saint Marie. Having ventured off into the wilderness alone and with no other people on the island apart from the trip participants, suspicion soon falls upon them. But with all of the course members providing one another with an alibi, how could any of them have committed the murder? That's what new detective inspector Neville intends to find out. Starring Ralf Little(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate in Dundee, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: The deal may have been signed and the name may have changed but it's business as usual for the Brexitcast team as Laura Kuenssberg, Chris Mason, Katya Adler and Adam Fleming deliver their usual mix of light-hearted gossip and serious analysis about the latest goings-on at Westminster(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: Fraud specialists seek a serial scam artist faking disability to claim thousands of pounds in insurance payouts, and also uncover the most prolific dental fraudster ever. Plus, a preacher turns prisoner when he's caught with his hand in the public purse. Presented by Michelle Ackerley(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Martell Maxwell explore a detached house in Strood in Kent, a prefab bungalow in Alvaston, Derby and a large semi-detached in Caterham, Surrey(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Derry-born Ciara McEleney wanted to move to Perth to give her daughters a sunshine lifestyle, but husband Ruairi had big concerns. One year on Nicki Chapman returns to find out what they decided to do(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers check takeaways in Oldham to ensure the food is safe to eat, making sure everyone gets home safely in Newcastle and cracking a gold bangle scam in Birmingham(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp hosts from Southwell Racecourse in Nottinghamshire, where experts Thomas Forrester and Tim Weeks help competing teams bag the best buys to make a profit at auction. Natasha also investigates a mystery item that dates back to the mid-19th century(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie helps a patient track down her virtual lover. Cupid's arrows are misfiring at the Mill - and no one would anticipate who has been messaging Joe online(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu delve into the ultra-competitive world of private tutoring, while a reunion with an old Rada friend, presents Sebastian with a choice about his future that could change everything for the agency. Tamzin Outhwaite guest stars, with Mark Benton and Jo Joyner(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a couple with a £650,000 budget to make the 500-mile move from a Scottish city to the English countryside around Wiltshire. Away from the house hunting, Jonnie joins a farmer, his dog and his flock for a husbandry masterclass with a unique breed of sheep native to the county(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Three passionate young farmers - the next generation of rising talent - battle it out at the Farmers Weekly Awards, to see who will be crowned Young Farmer of the Year. Narrated by Bill Paterson(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: As they head through the Cotswolds and on to Wales, Tim Medhurst is still trailing in the wake of old hand Christina Trevanion's winning streak. The newcomer gets to ring some very big bells while she calls in on a design museum and harks back to the utility furniture of the mid-20th century(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball host a Valentine's Day edition, featuring the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Host Tudur Owen relives the best of on-air entertainment, referencing the funniest, most fascinating clips from half a century and more of Welsh television. It's a story of music, magic and karaoke, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As the police investigate the break-in, Whitney is horrified when she realises her letter from Tony is missing - unaware Leo is lurking in Dot's room clutching the document. Ian is quick to confront Bobby about the social media posts but the troubled youngster realises his father is only worried about his own reputation. Wise words from Jean convince Mick to try to salvage his marriage(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Great Britain 400m hurdler Eilidh Doyle, Burnley goalkeeper Nick Pope, netball star Pamela Cookey and England rugby union international Zach Mercer join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell return for an episode of previously unseen material from the hit comedy panel show. Among the participants setting out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves are Clare Balding, Jay Blades, Liz Bonnin, Asim Chaudhry, Victoria Coren Mitchell, Greg Davies, Greg James, Sue Johnston, Guz Khan, Alice Levine, Steph McGovern, Stephen Merchant, Bob Mortimer, Sharon Osbourne, Richard Osman, Jennifer Saunders, Ranj Singh, Joe Sugg, Claudia Winkleman and Lucy Worsley(n)

programme.title: The Tuckers
programme.desc: Roberto finally moves in, and Nats throws a party to celebrate. The Tuckers do the catering, and Bobby has a huge decision to make. Comedy, starring Robert Pugh, Steve Spiers, Joshua McCord, Ben McGregor and Francois Pandolfo(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by singer, actor and producer Justin Timberlake and Oscar-nominated actress Anna Kendrick, who are both part of the voice cast of animated sequel Trolls World Tour. Singer-songwriter Sam Smith also appears, alongside comedian Alan Carr and Strictly and The Greatest Dancer star Oti Mabuse. Plus, more intrepid audience members attempt to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair(n)

programme.title: King Gary
programme.desc: The family heads to the coast for a holiday, with Gary keen to enjoy some quality time with Terri. However, she is obsessed with making holiday pals and befriends a French woman whose less-than-friendly husband has nothing in common with Gary - who finds himself relegated to looking after the kids while everyone else has fun. Big Gary is excited about getting a pasty from a shop he visited years ago, but can't quite remember its location, while Denise's fear of seagulls prevents her going down to the sea front to help. Comedy, starring Tom Davis, Laura Checkley, Simon Day and Camille Coduri(n)

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The queens' creative marketing skills are put to the test as they are tasked to create an advert and brand themselves. American drag queen Katya makes a surprise visit, while pop icon Cheryl joins Michelle Visage and Graham Norton on the judging panel(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: National and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45. Presented by Jon Kay and Tina Daheley(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Andrew Wong and Cyrus Todiwala, and a special guest faces his or her food heaven or hell(n)

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: The chef demonstrates how to make healthy dishes full of exciting flavours and textures, including Spanish-style squid and a chickpea and chorizo stew. Meanwhile, the group are invited to take part in a martial arts session, before Bev persuades the gang to try out her favourite workout, a vigourous dance class called Clubbercise. Later, Tom invites the whole gang over for lunch, where he prepares an easy chicken tray-bake, full of aromatic Moroccan flavours(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look back at last night's Premier League fixture between Wolverhampton Wanderers and Leicester City and preview Norwich City v Liverpool(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Thomas Plant help two teams find items at the Three Counties Showground in Malvern, Worcestershire, while presenter Eric Knowles oversees proceedings(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents live coverage of the prestigious meeting from Emirates Arena. World heptathlon champion Katarina Johnson-Thompson and Scottish favourite Laura Muir are among the stars scheduled to compete, with the latter aiming to break the world record in concluding event of the day - the rarely run 1,000m. With analysis from Denise Lewis, Colin Jackson and Paula Radcliffe, and commentary by Steve Cram, Andrew Cotter and Steve Backley(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: Doctor and television presenter Ranj Singh, former TOWIE cast member Jessica Wright, author and former politician Alan Johnson and comedian and writer Katy Brand brave the famous black chair answering questions on RuPaul's Drag Race, the character Monica Geller from Friends, pop group the Beatles and the movie Dirty Dancing. John Humphrys presents(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special experts edition of the general knowledge quiz. Contestants are Dr Zoe Williams, Dr Hilary Jones, Joe Swift, Rachel De Thame, Francis Wilson, Alex Beresford, Linda Barker and Judi James(n)

programme.title: The Greatest Dancer
programme.desc: Alesha Dixon and Jordan Banjo host the dancing contest, featuring performers from a variety of styles, including ballet, jazz, street, ballroom, contemporary and disco. Tonight, the competition continues as the remaining eight acts take to the stage, competing for a chance to showcase their skills on Strictly Come Dancing and take home the £50,000 prize(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. Eleven contestants start each show and compete across a range of five play-along-at-home games that will see one of them leave with a cash prize of up to £10,000. Hosted by Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ruby and Violette's relationship is pushed to the limit and Harmony's life is put in danger. Will struggles to finish a tedious report for Connie and turns to Lara for help, but he soon learns he should not mix his personal life with his work. Elsewhere, tensions run high when Ethan and Rash are forced to work together(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Norwich City v Liverpool at Carrow Road and Southampton v Burnley at Saint Mary's Stadium. Liverpool will have expected to extend their lead at the top of the table with victory over the struggling Canaries, while the much-improved Saints were looking to avenge a 3-0 defeat at the hands of the Clarets on the opening day of the season. Plus, the key moments from Friday night's clash between Wolverhampton and Leicester City at Molineux(n)

programme.title: Eagle Eye (2008)
programme.desc: Two strangers find themselves on the run from the authorities when they receive mysterious phone calls informing them their lives are in danger. They are forced to follow the instructions of a woman who is tracking their every move, while at the same time trying to discover who is behind the conspiracy. Thriller, starring Shia LaBeouf, Michelle Monaghan, Rosario Dawson and Michael Chiklis(n)

Meanwhile, the group are invited to take part in a martial arts session, before Bev persuades the gang to try out her favourite workout, a vigourous dance class called Clubbercise. Later, Tom invites the whole gang over for lunch, where he prepares an easy chicken tray-bake, full of aromatic Moroccan flavours(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look back at last night's Premier League fixture between Wolverhampton Wanderers and Leicester City and preview Norwich City v Liverpool(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Thomas Plant help two teams find items at the Three Counties Showground in Malvern, Worcestershire, while presenter Eric Knowles oversees proceedings(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents live coverage of the prestigious meeting from Emirates Arena. World heptathlon champion Katarina Johnson-Thompson and Scottish favourite Laura Muir are among the stars scheduled to compete, with the latter aiming to break the world record in concluding event of the day - the rarely run 1,000m. With analysis from Denise Lewis, Colin Jackson and Paula Radcliffe, and commentary by Steve Cram, Andrew Cotter and Steve Backley(n)

programme.title: Sportscene Results
programme.desc: David Currie presents all the results and news from today's matches across the country(n)

programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special experts edition of the general knowledge quiz. Contestants are Dr Zoe Williams, Dr Hilary Jones, Joe Swift, Rachel De Thame, Francis Wilson, Alex Beresford, Linda Barker and Judi James(n)

programme.title: The Greatest Dancer
programme.desc: Alesha Dixon and Jordan Banjo host the dancing contest, featuring performers from a variety of styles, including ballet, jazz, street, ballroom, contemporary and disco. Tonight, the competition continues as the remaining eight acts take to the stage, competing for a chance to showcase their skills on Strictly Come Dancing and take home the £50,000 prize(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. Eleven contestants start each show and compete across a range of five play-along-at-home games that will see one of them leave with a cash prize of up to £10,000. Hosted by Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ruby and Violette's relationship is pushed to the limit and Harmony's life is put in danger. Will struggles to finish a tedious report for Connie and turns to Lara for help, but he soon learns he should not mix his personal life with his work. Elsewhere, tensions run high when Ethan and Rash are forced to work together(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Norwich City v Liverpool at Carrow Road and Southampton v Burnley at Saint Mary's Stadium. Liverpool will have expected to extend their lead at the top of the table with victory over the struggling Canaries, while the much-improved Saints were looking to avenge a 3-0 defeat at the hands of the Clarets on the opening day of the season. Plus, the key moments from Friday night's clash between Wolverhampton and Leicester City at Molineux(n)

programme.title: Eagle Eye (2008)
programme.desc: Two strangers find themselves on the run from the authorities when they receive mysterious phone calls informing them their lives are in danger. They are forced to follow the instructions of a woman who is tracking their every move, while at the same time trying to discover who is behind the conspiracy. Thriller, starring Shia LaBeouf, Michelle Monaghan, Rosario Dawson and Michael Chiklis(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development of a terraced house in Ramsbottom in Bury, a former shop in Gravesend in Kent and a house in Newcastle-under-Lyme(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Carl and Danielle want to move to Australia but a health scare has shown them how fickle life can be. A week in Perth helps them decide whether they could leave their family behind(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: The officers investigate the sale of knives to under-18s. They also catch up with a Lamborghini-driving fraudster who has so many aliases he loses track of who he is supposed to be(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: A visit from an old friend forces Karen to confront her past. A toy gun battle for Joe's birthday becomes a little too real and Bear tries to get in on Ayesha and Valerie's party(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu are hired to locate a photograph stolen from a local pub, whose owner is in the middle of a feud with another local family - only for events to take a deadly turn(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a house hunting couple find a country home with a budget of £700,000 in Buckinghamshire, and later visits a farm that produces high-end snail caviar(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Shrewsbury Food Festival plays host to first-generation farmer Sam, with his rare breed of pork, and the family run Kerry Vale Vineyard, selling their English wine(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The search reaches the New Forest, and after an encounter with some ponies, Tim Medhurst buys a horse, while Christina Trevanion turns farrier. Other items include a George III commode and some Scottish treasure(n)

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mick regrets admitting to Linda that Ollie might be better off without her, but before he can apologise his wife storms out. Whitney goes to the Slaters' to apologise to Kat, giving Leo the chance he needs to search for the letter. A night out for the Albert Square youngsters start badly as Dennis is less than pleased to see Bobby - and things quickly go downhill from there(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman presents the semi-final, which sees the four remaining cooks produce a selection of party canapes and rustle up a dish from a tinned store cupboard staple. The ones who impress the least must fight for their place in the final by cooking again in the eliminator - and this week, Angela Hartnett gets them to produce a recipe also happens to be her husband's favourite dessert. Who will be hanging up their apron at the last hurdle?(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A desert island survival course goes wrong when the leader - a trained survival expert and ex-SAS soldier - is found dead in the forest of a remote island near Saint Marie. Having ventured off into the wilderness alone and with no other people on the island apart from the trip participants, suspicion soon falls upon them. But with all of the course members providing one another with an alibi, how could any of them have committed the murder? That's what new detective inspector Neville intends to find out. Starring Ralf Little(n)

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers presents a review of the week's political news, including reports on the latest events at Stormont and Westminster. Plus, comment and analysis with studio guests(n)

programme.title: Question Time

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: Fraud specialists seek a serial scam artist faking disability to claim thousands of pounds in insurance payouts, and also uncover the most prolific dental fraudster ever. Plus, a preacher turns prisoner when he's caught with his hand in the public purse. Presented by Michelle Ackerley(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Martell Maxwell explore a detached house in Strood in Kent, a prefab bungalow in Alvaston, Derby and a large semi-detached in Caterham, Surrey(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Derry-born Ciara McEleney wanted to move to Perth to give her daughters a sunshine lifestyle, but husband Ruairi had big concerns. One year on Nicki Chapman returns to find out what they decided to do(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers check takeaways in Oldham to ensure the food is safe to eat, making sure everyone gets home safely in Newcastle and cracking a gold bangle scam in Birmingham(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu delve into the ultra-competitive world of private tutoring, while a reunion with an old Rada friend, presents Sebastian with a choice about his future that could change everything for the agency. Tamzin Outhwaite guest stars, with Mark Benton and Jo Joyner(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a couple with a £650,000 budget to make the 500-mile move from a Scottish city to the English countryside around Wiltshire. programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Three passionate young farmers - the next generation of rising talent - battle it out at the Farmers Weekly Awards, to see who will be crowned Young Farmer of the Year. Narrated by Bill Paterson(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: As they head through the Cotswolds and on to Wales, Tim Medhurst is still trailing in the wake of old hand Christina Trevanion's winning streak. The newcomer gets to ring some very big bells while she calls in on a design museum and harks back to the utility furniture of the mid-20th century(n)

programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers face an emotional job as they help a Worcester couple redesign their garden in memory of their son, who died at the age of 26. Andy and Angela have a budget of £5,000 and want to recapture some of the happy times they spent together, creating a peaceful space to relax and reflect in(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As the police investigate the break-in, Whitney is horrified when she realises her letter from Tony is missing - unaware Leo is lurking in Dot's room clutching the document. Ian is quick to confront Bobby about the social media posts but the troubled youngster realises his father is only worried about his own reputation. Wise words from Jean convince Mick to try to salvage his marriage(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?

programme.title: King Gary
programme.desc: The family heads to the coast for a holiday, with Gary keen to enjoy some quality time with Terri. However, she is obsessed with making holiday pals and befriends a French woman whose less-than-friendly husband has nothing in common with Gary - who finds himself relegated to looking after the kids while everyone else has fun. Big Gary is excited about getting a pasty from a shop he visited years ago, but can't quite remember its location, while Denise's fear of seagulls prevents her going down to the sea front to help. Comedy, starring Tom Davis, Laura Checkley, Simon Day and Camille Coduri(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Callie Lewis was just 24 when she took her own life. She had been in the care of the NHS, but fell through the cracks of an understaffed and overstretched system. For the last 16 months, Panorama has been filming with her family as they tried to understand what went wrong. Shortly before she died, they learned that Callie was a member of an online suicide forum, where she found detailed advice on how to kill herself. As reporter Ellie Flynn discovers, Callie's death comes at a time when many people with mental health issues continue to complain about their struggle to connect with the services they need(n)

programme.title: Our World
programme.desc: Panzuto was a mafia boss, and a hardened killer. For years he played a key role in Naples' Camorra, but now he has turned state witness and is helping put his former associates behind bars. Dominic Casciani gains exclusive access to an Italian anti-mafia prison to meet Panzuto and hear his story of love, murder and betrayal. It's a tale which took him from the back streets of Naples to a caravan park in Blackpool. We hear from those at the sharp end of the ongoing battle against organised crime in Naples, and from those who believe the tide has finally turned. Above all we hear from the man himself: why did he decide to break with the Camorra, and what does the future hold for him?(n)

programme.title: The Greek Islands with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia reaches the island of Chios, where her family's story began. She's joined by her mother Chrissi and together they explore the home of their ancestors. In search of the fascinating history of Mastica, a natural resin only found on Chios, they visit a farm to discover how this mystical and miraculous tree sap has been harvested for thousands of years. After following in the footsteps of legendary Greek poet Homer, who is reputed to have been born on the island, their trip comes to an emotional end when they track down the house where Julia's great-grandparents once lived(n)

programme.desc: A Frenchwoman arrives to help with preparations for Aidensfield's twinning with her home town, but things go wrong when she is left to the tender mercies of Peggy. Rob marks his return to the village by saving a con-man after his car crashes into the river, while district nurse Carol Cassidy struggles to treat a woman with an over-protective husband. Jonathan Kerrigan and Lisa Kay star(n)

programme.desc: Kim and Frank talk about Chris's affair with Rachel, Eric pesters Betty for money from the rock concert. Rachel enlists help from Jessica to stop the hunt at Home Farm(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth sets out to solve the baffling murder of a Cambridge don who met with foul play shortly after deciding to change his will. Starring David Suchet, Beth Goddard and Mark Kingston(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A Frenchwoman arrives to help with preparations for Aidensfield's twinning with her home town, but things go wrong when she is left to the tender mercies of Peggy. Jonathan Kerrigan and Lisa Kay star(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Schoolgirl Jennifer Sutcliffe becomes embroiled in a mystery when priceless jewels are hidden among her belongings. The precious stones once belonged to a prince who fell foul of a revolution, and now his cousin is a pupil at the same school as Jennifer, where Poirot finds himself drawn into the unlikely situation and realises nothing is quite what it seems. David Suchet stars as the Belgian sleuth, with Harriet Walter and Claire Skinner(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis investigate the murder of a businesswoman connected with an organisation that supports gifted children, who had apparently been blackmailing the father of one particularly promising youngster. Further deaths follow, and as the body count rises, they realise there is a decades-old secret at the heart of the case. Matters are complicated still further by the presence in Oxford of former MI5 spymaster Grace Orde, who is visiting to sign copies of her tell-all memoir - which provides a vital clue to the killer's identity. Detective drama, guest starring Anna Chancellor and Cherie Lunghi, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Still reeling from the death of one of their own, Ronnie and Joe face a new challenge when an innocent young mum is fatally stabbed in a busy marketplace, just as a senior police officer publicly pledges to crack down on knife crime. Under pressure from all sides, the pair struggle to gather evidence against the youth they think is responsible, and the suspect's unintentional confession appears to bring the case to a close. However, Ronnie is the only person who hears the admission, and it proves difficult getting anyone to believe him. With the detective's honesty cast into doubt and his effectiveness questioned at every turn, is this the end of his career? With Tony Gardner, Sharon Small and Colin Salmon(n)

programme.title: Liar
programme.desc: Laura travels to Edinburgh to try to uncover the truth behind Andrew's wife's death, but finds more than she bargained for. Meanwhile, tensions between Tom and Katy reach breaking point as their secret threatens to surface. Thriller, starring Joanne Froggatt and Ioan Gruffudd(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Captain Hastings and the Belgian supersleuth investigate a baffling gangland murder which draws them both into the underworld of London's Little Italy. Miss Lemon turns out to have a link with the victim's valet. Whodunit, starring David Suchet and Hugh Fraser(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford sleuth investigates a double mystery connected to the local Greek community - the death of a restaurant chef and the disappearance of a baby - and wonders whether an academic event attended by some of the university's leading classical scholars could shed light on the case. Mystery, starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Jan Harvey, Martin Jarvis and James Hazeldine(n)
